authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.21 - Condition of participation: Quality assessment and performance improvement program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital rule for quality assessment and performance improvement. It supports service-line quality dependency tracking while reserving formal QAPI authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality dependency and administrative readiness tracking

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical record regulation for prompt completion, authentication, retention, and required record content. It anchors record readiness and official-record boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: medical record completion, authentication, retention, and official-record boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review rule. It supports escalation when administrative record handling would cross into admission, continued stay, professional service, utilization, or medical-necessity review.

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. This guidance defines treatment, payment, and health care operations contexts relevant to service-line administrative evidence use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling for treatment, payment, and health care operations

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI uses, disclosures, and requests. It directly constrains PHI-bearing service-line packets and status summaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Record Maintenance & Access Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS provider guidance for maintaining, retaining, furnishing, and granting access to medical records. It supports record location, access, and audit-readiness handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record maintenance, access, retention, retrieval, and audit readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medical Record Documentation Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS MLN guidance for documentation sufficiency, missing records, orders, and signatures. It grounds evidence packet completeness checks for payment-review and service-line administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: documentation sufficiency, missing records, orders, signatures, and attachment readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS prior authorization workflow guidance for specified hospital outpatient department services. It supports authorization status and attachment dependency tracking without granting payer interpretation authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: prior authorization and hospital outpatient service dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is designated by HHS as the national operating rule authoring entity for HIPAA administrative transactions. It grounds eligibility, benefits, prior authorization, claim, remittance, and attachment transaction terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, benefits, prior authorization, claim, remittance, and attachment transaction awareness

authoritative_sources.title: Workflow Assessment for Health IT Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and health IT workflow authority. The toolkit supports workflow mapping, administrative workflow impact, constraint identification, and handoff design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workflow mapping, blocker identification, handoff design, and throughput status

authoritative_sources.title: HL7 FHIR R4 ServiceRequest
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is the recognized health interoperability standards body. ServiceRequest grounds vendor-neutral terminology for orders, referrals, procedure requests, and service dependencies across service lines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order, referral, task, service dependency, and EHR request normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Fundamentals of the Legal Health Record and Designated Record Set
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA is the recognized HIM professional body. This practice brief supports legal health record, designated record set, source-system, retention, and disclosure-readiness terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legal health record, designated record set, source-system mapping, retention, and disclosure readiness

workflow_stages.name: Service-line request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request by service line, requested output, patient/member or aggregate context, administrative action, authority requested, and adjacent-owner implications.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Tenant fact, source-system, and access validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate facility, service line, care setting, payer, plan, patient/member status, service dates, source hierarchy, role-based access, retention, privacy, and approval owners before administrative handling.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record and prerequisite evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Check orders, encounter documentation, consents, eligibility, benefits, referrals, authorizations, attachments, case-management notes, discharge or transition records, charge/coding/claim artifacts, remittances, denials, and appeals for completeness, currency, and source traceability.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and status production
workflow_stages.description: Maintain administrative notes, document completeness summaries, packet state, line readiness, blocker status, owner, due date, throughput state, quality dependency, material/readiness constraint, and audit trail.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Retention access and governance control
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant retention, access, consent, legal hold, role-based access, minimum-necessary, and PHI handling constraints before packet assembly or routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff escalation or refusal
workflow_stages.description: Route clinical, utilization, payer, coding, privacy, compliance, legal, finance, staffing, safety, release, and official-record decisions to the correct owner when authority shifts or confidence is insufficient.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: retention, access, or governance escalation package
production status summary with line state, blockers, quality dependencies, material/readiness constraints, and due dates
charge capture, coding-support, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and attachment documentation
handoff note and owner acceptance record
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
HIM, document management, legal health record, and designated record set repositories
charge capture, coding, claims, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
quality, compliance, privacy, legal hold, and audit systems

allowed: classify the request as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or requiring orchestrator return
validate tenant facts, source records, system links, retention/access posture, and prerequisite evidence needed for administrative handling
maintain administrative records, status notes, completeness summaries, readiness trackers, blocker logs, and audit-trail summaries
prepare evidence-backed next-step packages, production status summaries, and handoff notes without overstating authority
determine whether records are complete, current, properly classified, and ready for the requested administrative use under tenant rules
escalate: clinical, patient-safety, diagnosis, treatment, discharge readiness, or licensed review questions
coverage, authorization applicability, referral validity, payer-policy interpretation, medical necessity, claim disposition, or appeal strategy
coding assignment, coding validation, CDI, charge correction, write-off, or financial approval
PHI disclosure approval, privacy-law interpretation, minimum-necessary conflict, access conflict, legal hold, retention conflict, or official-record decision
source-system conflict, policy conflict, missing source precedence, missing owner, or missing approval authority
refuse: altering, deleting, backdating, concealing, certifying, or signing official health record content
releasing or marking a packet ready without required evidence, owner acceptance, approvals, or audit trail

diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical judgment, medical necessity, coding, payer-policy interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, legal advice, financial approval, staffing approval, safety-critical authority, or policy override
uncontrolled PHI export or distribution without requester authority, role-based access, approved channel, and minimum-necessary scope

domain_failure_modes: Patient or member harm from bad routing, missing clinical escalation, or incorrect administrative assumptions.
PHI exposure or privacy violation from uncontrolled service-line packet assembly or patient-level export.
Incomplete documentation trail from unsupported readiness or release status.
Revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure from missing eligibility, referral, authorization, order, documentation, coding-support, claim, or attachment evidence.
Improper official-record handling from unauthorized amendment, deletion, certification, or signoff.
Service-line throughput or release error from missing owner acceptance, quality dependency, material/readiness evidence, or approval authority.
Policy conflict masked by generic synthesis instead of compliance, legal, privacy, payer, or tenant owner escalation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific medical record retention and state Medicaid documentation rules vary by jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack treats jurisdiction, state Medicaid, payer contract, tenant retention policy, and legal hold status as required runtime facts. No blocking ambiguity remains for the public validated baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Service-line packet criteria differ by tenant, department, payer, and care setting.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires tenant packet criteria, RACI, source hierarchy, approval matrix, and release criteria before producing readiness or completeness outputs.

notes: The research output constrains the pack to administrative records, documentation, status, readiness, and handoff work.
The lane stops before clinical, payer, coding, utilization, privacy, legal, financial, safety-critical, release, or official-record adjudication.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
